title: fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. subintegerrima
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. subintegerrima, also known as the lancet-leaf green ash, is a subspecies of the green ash tree native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of Fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. subintegerrima may be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
